title: athlete — retention windows
description: Active + paused athletes never expire. Graduated athletes retain indefinitely (alumni relations) unless the tenant opts into graduate_purge_after_years. Withdrawn + archived athletes enter a two-phase retention: 90-day grace hold on emergency_contact_* + medical_* fields (GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) minimisation), then 7-year retention on the demographic + status metadata (SOC 2 CC6.3 + safeguarding retention + national medical-record retention). Medical history rows retain 7 years even after the parent Athlete is hard-deleted — migrated to a compliance-archive table for the residual window. Same pattern as staff_compensation_history in the staff module.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(c) — data minimisation (medical + emergency contact redaction at 90 days)
gdpr Art. 8 — parental consent for children (retention aligns with consent-recorded_at + 12mo re-consent cadence when workflow entitlement on)
gdpr Art. 9 — special-category data (regulated_health-tier for medical fields)
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with medical-retention Art. 17(3)(b) exception)
soc2 CC6.3 — segregation of duties audit trail retention
national medical-record law — 7-year retention floor (US HIPAA, EU medical device regulation, UK GDPR Art. 9 supplementary Act)
coppa — US children under 13, 30-day retention post-consent-revocation (special-case)

purge.notes: Athletes in non-terminal states are the living roster. Retention only applies once status IN ('graduated', 'withdrawn', 'archived').

hot.days: indefinite unless graduate_purge_after_years is set (default 0 = off)

purge.action: transition to archived, then continue through the archive-retention pipeline
purge.notes: Most tenants retain graduated athletes for alumni relations. Some may opt into a 10 / 15-year auto-purge for privacy-conscious tenants.

hot.description: emergency_contact_name + emergency_contact_phone + emergency_contact_relationship + medical_conditions + medical_allergies + medical_medications + medical_notes

purge.trigger: status IN ('withdrawn', 'archived') AND deleted_at + 90 days (or withdrawn_at + 90 days if archived without soft-delete)
purge.action: field-level redaction (replace with [REDACTED]) via PurgeArchivedAthletesJob phase 1
purge.notes: Preserves the rest of the row for the 7-year retention. Only the confidential + regulated_health sub-fields are redacted. The audit trail keeps the pre-redaction values in the athlete_medical_history table with 7-year retention (regulated-access only).

purge.trigger: > 7 years past deleted_at + no downstream references (AthleteEnrollment hard-purged, Membership hard-purged)
purge.notes: RESTRICT FKs catch active references; the job records `retention_blocked` metric when hard-delete is refused. Retention runs against deleted_at, not created_at — the compliance clock starts at archival.

cold.days: 2555 (7 years) — outlives the parent Athlete hard-purge if necessary

purge.notes: PARENT-INDEPENDENT retention. When a tenant is erased (TenantErased), medical_history rows migrate to a compliance-archive table (retained in encrypted form by ops for the residual 7-year window). This is a regulatory-compliance requirement — medical-record retention outlives the tenant relationship. Same pattern as staff_compensation_history.
Guardian rows have no independent retention — they exist only in service of the parent athlete.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention. This applies to normal audits + the dedicated athlete_medical_history stream.

TenantErased cascades hard-delete of every Athlete row via FK CASCADE, but the audit rows + medical_history rows survive the cascade (per audit module's outlives-source design + the compliance-archive migration for medical history).

Athletes turning 18 via CheckAthleteMinorTurnedAdultJob: prior guardian-recorded consents auto-expire (metadata.consent_re_record_required=true) — the retention on those historical consents follows the audit-log 7-year rule; the LIVE consent snapshot rolls forward at re-consent.
